metadata.description: Offline high-throughput bulk ingest + ETL: import a deterministic LDBC-SNB-like social network from CSV via the real graphus-bulk binary, characterise ingest throughput + the on-disk store footprint / amplification, and prove a lossless import -> dump -> re-import round-trip.

notes: Ingest throughput + CPU/RAM/time are the REAL `graphus-bulk import` child process (pid metered by polling its /proc or ps) over a 0.0380s offline load of 4829 elements (127218.9 elements/sec, 3.444 MB/sec of input CSV). These are machine-variant and are NOT gated.

storage.store_bytes / store_pages / wal_bytes / wal_pages are the durable graph.store image + retained graph.wal redo log measured for the SAME dataset (from bulk_storage's storage.json). storage.space_amplification = on-disk STORE bytes-per-node and storage.write_amplification = on-disk STORE bytes-per-edge — the DETERMINISTIC per-element costs the baseline gate holds to a tight band. The CSV-relative amplifications (store/total/write vs logical_csv_bytes) are in the workload params for human visibility.
Round-trip is LOSSLESS: import -> dump -> re-import preserves the id-independent content hash f09ef9edcd4584631cc07af0116a0d22 (proven by bulk_roundtrip).

COMMITTED BASELINE (rmp #270): the fast-profile reference run for the regression gate. The offline import is fully DETERMINISTIC (store_bytes/store_pages/wal_bytes/wal_pages are byte-identical across runs and hosts). Only STABLE STRUCTURAL metrics are gated by bulk_baseline_cmp — the dataset size (dataset.nodes/relationships), the imported_elements count, and the durable store footprint (storage.store_bytes/wal_bytes within 15%, storage.space_amplification = store bytes-per-node and storage.write_amplification = store bytes-per-edge within 15%). Ingest throughput/CPU/RAM/wall-time are machine-variant and are NOT gated. Recorded on linux/x86_64.
